case_title: NATHANAEL RICHARD KREVOR VS. GEORGE VALVERDE, DIRECTOR STATE OF CALIFORNIA,
cause_of_action: WRITS OF MANDATE OR PROH., CERTI., ETC./ADMIN. AGEN

docket_entries.description: NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER STAYIING SUSPENSION OF DRIVING PRIVILEGE FILED BY PETITIONER KREVOR, NATHANAEL RICHARD
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR HEARING ON PETITIONER'S WRIT OF MANDATE HEARING SET FOR MAY-29-2012 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302
MINI MINUTES FOR MAY-29-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
MINI MINUTES FOR JUN-19-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER NATHANAEL RICHARD KREVOR'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS DENIED. THE WEIGHT OF THE EVIDENCE SUPPORTS THE UNDERLYING DECISION. PETITIONER'S CONDITIONAL ACCEPTANCE OF THE BREATH TEST CAN BE CONSIDERED A REFUSAL AND LATER COOPERATION WITH THE BLOOD TEST DOES NOT MITIGATE OR OBVIATE THE REPERCUSSIONS OF THIS REFUSAL. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: JAMES MCBRIDE; COURT REPORTER: TEANNA L. WARD, CSR NO. 119118

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER'S WRIT OF MANDATE IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO JUNE 19, 2012. PARTIES ARE REQUIRED TO COMPLY WITH RULE OF COURT 3.1140 AND PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF THE PORTIONS OF THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD RELIED UPON IN THEIR PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302 NO LATER THAN 4:00PM ON JUNE 10 WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE. JUDGE KAHN DISCLOSES THAT MR. BAIR, ONE OF THE ATTORNEYS FOR PETITIONER, IS THE HUSBAND OF AN EMPLOYEE OF THE COURT, YET THIS FACT DOES NOT AFFECT JUDGE KAHN'S ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL TO BOTH SIDES. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

calendar.matters:
Hearing On Petitioner'S Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Jun-19-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOHN J. PETRY OF DUI DEFENSE EXPERTS, TEL. NO. (510)645-0027 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. KAREN L. DONALD, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, TEL. NO. (510)622-2202 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Petitioner Nathanael Richard Krevor's Petition for Writ Of Mandate is Denied. The weight of the evidence supports the underlying decision. Petitioner's conditional acceptance of the breath test can be considered a refusal and later cooperation with the blood test does not mitigate or obviate the repercussions of this refusal. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: James Mcbride; Court Reporter: Teanna L. Ward, CSR No. 119118 = (302/JJM)

Hearing On Petitioner'S Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar May-29-2012 Continued To Jun-19-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Petitioner's Writ Of Mandate is continued on the court's own motion to June 19, 2012. Parties are required to comply with Rule of Court 3.1140 and provide courtesy copies of the portions of the administrative record relied upon in their papers to department 302 no later than 4:00pm on June 10 with a cover letter stating the new hearing date. Judge Kahn discloses that Mr. Bair, one of the attorneys for petitioner, is the husband of an employee of the court, yet this fact does not affect Judge Kahn's ability to be fair and impartial to both sides. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077 =(302/HEK)
